policy_sections.content: Second graders are expected to read nightly and complete short practice assignments. Homework should typically take no more than 30 minutes per night on school days. If your child is consistently spending more than 30 minutes on homework, please contact the classroom teacher to adjust assignments.
Second grade uses a standards-based report card. Students receive marks of 4 (Exceeds Standard), 3 (Meets Standard), 2 (Approaching Standard), or 1 (Beginning) for each learning target.
Late work is accepted within one week of the original due date. Families will be notified if late work becomes a recurring concern.
Third graders should expect approximately 35–40 minutes of homework per night, including independent reading.
First graders typically have 15–20 minutes of homework per night, largely focused on reading and simple practice tasks.
Homework in kindergarten is optional and should not exceed 10–15 minutes per night. Families are encouraged to read together daily.
Fourth graders may have up to 45 minutes of homework per night, including reading and content review.
Fifth graders should plan for 50–60 minutes of homework most nights, with occasional larger projects.
Homework is intended to reinforce classwork, build responsibility, and strengthen home–school connections. Families are encouraged to provide a quiet space and routine for homework completion.
Students may retake certain unit assessments after completing corrections and additional practice as assigned by the teacher.

staff_members.bio: Ms. Chang has taught 3rd grade math for nearly two decades and loves helping students build confidence with numbers.
Mr. Hernandez focuses on real-world problem solving and math games to keep students engaged.
Ms. Kim integrates hands-on science projects into her math instruction whenever possible.
Mr. Lee teaches all core subjects in a 2nd-grade homeroom and enjoys partnering with families.
Ms. Rivera leads the 4th Grade Math Puzzles Club and loves project-based learning.
Mr. Nguyen coaches the Science Olympiad team.
Ms. Patel organizes many of the 1st grade family events.
Principal Smith has served as the instructional leader at Lincoln Elementary for 5 years.
Mr. Brown directs the school choir and leads the Music & Movement after-school program.
Ms. Lopez runs the after-school art club for 4th grade.
Mr. Gomez leads the Science Explorers club.
Ms. Wilson supports student wellbeing and social-emotional learning.

summer_camp_sessions.name: STEM Explorers Camp

summer_camp_sessions.name: Kindergarten Readiness Camp
summer_camp_sessions.description: Helps incoming kindergarteners get comfortable with school routines.
summer_camp_sessions.activities_summary: Play-based learning, songs, and center time.
